fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Pennsylvania Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to pennsylvania, respiratory therapist compact pennsylvania, pennsylvania respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Pennsylvania Board of Medicine — RT
board.mailing_address:
PA State Board of Medicine
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Create a PALS Account
steps.description: Navigate to PALS.pa.gov and select 'New Applicants' to register for an individual licensing account.

steps.title: Start Your Application
steps.description: Log in to your PALS account, click on 'New Application,' and select 'Respiratory Therapist' from the available license types to begin.

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within the PALS system, providing accurate personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for official submission of all supporting documents, including official transcripts directly from your accredited respiratory therapy program and your NBRC examination score reports, as specified in your PALS application.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee through the PALS system using a credit card to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your PALS account for updates on your application's status and promptly respond to any requests for additional information from the Pennsylvania State Board of Medicine.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Pennsylvania respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: Respiratory Therapists seeking initial licensure or licensure by endorsement in Pennsylvania must apply through the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS).

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Visit PALS.pa.gov, click on "New Applicants" under "Individual Licensing Services," and follow the prompts to create an account with a valid email address and password.
